item.title: Anthropic CEO to meet White House chief of staff as US seeks access to Mythos model
item.description: Dario Amodei due to speak with Susie Wiles despite ongoing lawsuits over whether AI lab is national security threat

item.title: EU prepares for jet fuel sharing as supplies dwindle
item.description: Energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen says Europe moving towards supply crisis “very rapidly”

item.title: Lloyd’s of London to end flagship diversity event
item.description: Insurance marketplace has for years faced claims of sexual harassment and inappropriate workplace behaviour

item.title: Total comp: $4.6mn. Of which furniture discounts? Uh, $900k
item.description: Digging into Arhaus’s compensation tables

item.title: Richard Desmond loses £1.3bn lawsuit over National Lottery contract
item.description: Media tycoon’s Northern & Shell argued regulator had favoured Allwyn in award process

item.title: Anthropic chief Dario Amodei: ‘I don’t want AI turned on our own people’
item.description: The tech entrepreneur on Claude Mythos, repercussions from the Pentagon dispute — and his message for the super-rich

item.title: Green energy’s fossil fuel problem
item.description: Sulphur squeeze highlights sector’s exposure to the industry it seeks to replace

item.title: What is a city without children?
item.description: As urban housebuilding strategies around the world increasingly push families out, the ‘sidewalk ballet’ — and heart — of community life is under threat

item.title: Rivian pitches big carmakers on software deals
item.description: Electric truck start-up’s CEO says it is ‘inconceivable’ traditional groups can keep market share without better software

item.title: Threat of ‘unprecedented’ Spurs relegation dominates Premier League run-in
item.description: Despite being one of English football’s Big Six and among Europe’s richest clubs, Tottenham are heading for the drop

item.title: Deutsche Bank alerts regulators to potential Russia sanctions lapses
item.description: German bank found it had accepted deposits of more than €100,000 from individuals subject to EU sanctions restrictions

item.title: Is it wrong to beg for freebies?
item.description: What’s a Binky? And isn’t blagging part of the job of an influencer?

item.title: Energy independence could make America more aggressive
item.description: Being a net exporter means that energy shocks no longer constrain US foreign policy in the ways they once did

item.title: London office landlord Workspace warns of ‘substantial’ profit hit
item.description: Squeeze from lower rents and higher costs forces company to say it will cut dividend

item.title: Shares in world’s second-biggest train maker plunge on profit downgrade
item.description: Alstom cites slow progress on some projects and scraps free cash flow target

item.title: Adani overtakes Ambani as Asia’s richest man after Iran war hits oil
item.description: Middle East conflict weighs on Ambani’s petrochemicals-dependent empire

item.title: French telecoms groups raise takeover offer for Patrick Drahi’s SFR
item.description: Deal would be a key test of antitrust regulators’ views on in-market consolidation

item.title: Uber expands its stake in Delivery Hero in €270mn deal
item.description: German delivery group’s largest shareholder Prosus has agreed to reduce its stake over EU competition concerns

item.title: Energy crisis relief must support green goals, says EU climate chief
item.description: Also in this newsletter: EU lawmakers clash over bank capital rules

item.title: FirstFT: Mandelson fiasco takes down UK Foreign Office official
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Israel-Hizbollah ceasefire and German economy stagnates

item.title: Jardines and CK Hutchison pursue Hong Kong supermarket megadeal
item.description: Merger of ParknShop with Wellcome would create a dominant player in the territory’s food retail market

item.title: Europe embraces mega M&A
item.description: The EU is planning its biggest relaxation of corporate merger rules in decades

item.title: Latest AI models could threaten world banking system, financial officials warn
item.description: Anthropic’s new Claude Mythos Preview model could expose vulnerabilities in lenders’ cyber defences

item.title: Wall Street banks start trading derivatives to bet on pain in private credit
item.description: JPMorgan and Barclays are among those offering CDS on Apollo, Ares and Blackstone funds

item.title: Merger rules to favour scale and innovation, says EU competition chief
item.description: Teresa Ribera confirms consumer interest will no longer be main criterion in approving or rejecting corporate deals

item.title: UK farmers face tough choices as war pushes up costs
item.description: Fertiliser and fuel prices have risen against a backdrop of depressed wheat and milk markets

item.title: Why Coutts is making an unexpected play for video games
item.description: The over 300-year-old lender is touting its status as a major banker to the sector

item.title: Recruiters hit by hiring slowdown in Europe
item.description: PageGroup chief warns Iran war has heightened uncertainty in hiring outlook for the rest of the year

item.title: OnlyFans nears deal to sell minority stake at more than $3bn valuation
item.description: Discussions with Architect Capital come less than a month after the UK-based platform’s owner Leonid Radvinsky died

item.title: Humanoid robot chases wild boars off the streets of Warsaw
item.description: Video of Rolex-wearing robot goes viral as it becomes Poland’s first robotic influencer on social media
